docs.title: RAGKit
docs.text:
Universal RAG framework SDK for Python.

RAGKit gives you a complete Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline in a few lines of code. Plug in your data sources — RAGKit handles chunking, embedding, vector storage, retrieval, and LLM generation.

docs.title: Why RAGKit?
docs.text:
Building a RAG system from scratch means assembling a dozen libraries, writing the same boilerplate pipeline in every project, and debugging the gaps between them. RAGKit collapses that into a single, composable SDK.

from ragkit import Agent, MarkdownSource

agent = Agent()
agent.use(MarkdownSource("./docs"))
agent.index()

print(agent.ask("How does authentication work?"))

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Your Data Sources
      ↓
  Source Loader  →  Cleaner  →  Chunker  →  Embedder  →  Vector Store
                                                               ↓
Your Question  →  Embedder  →  Retriever  →  Prompt Builder  →  OpenRouter  →  Answer

Every component is replaceable. Swap the vector store, the embedder, the model — RAGKit's interfaces keep everything wired together.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Sources Markdown, TXT, JSON, CSV, REST API, Directory Embeddings Sentence Transformers (local), more via plugins Vector stores Chroma (default), Qdrant LLMs OpenRouter — 100+ models in a single API Streaming Async token streaming Cache Embedding cache + query cache (disk-based) Hooks before_generate, after_generate, after_response Plugins pip install ragkit-pdf pattern CLI rag init / index / sync / ask / dev / serve

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Quick Start

    Get your first agent running in 5 minutes.

  • Configuration

    Full reference for rag.yaml and environment variables.

  • Sources

    Connect Markdown, CSV, REST APIs, and more.

  • Pipeline

    How chunking, embedding, and indexing work.

  • Models

    Configure OpenRouter and choose your LLM.

  • CLI

    Complete CLI command reference.

  • Plugins

    Build and publish your own RAGKit plugin.

  • API Reference

    Full Python API documentation.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Complete Python API reference for RAGKit.

docs.title: Agent
docs.text:
The main entry point. Orchestrates sources, pipeline, retrieval, and generation.

from ragkit import Agent

agent = Agent(config="rag.yaml")

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
Agent(config: str | Path | ProjectConfig = "rag.yaml")
Parameter Type Description config str, Path, or ProjectConfig Config file path or config object

docs.title: .use(source) → Agent
docs.text:
Add a data source. Returns self for chaining.

agent.use(MarkdownSource("./docs"))
      .use(CsvSource("./faq.csv"))

docs.title: .configure(...) → Agent
docs.text:
Update pipeline and retrieval settings. Returns self.

agent.configure(
    chunk_size=300,   # int | None
    overlap=30,       # int | None
    top_k=10,         # int | None
)

docs.title: .set_model(provider) → Agent
docs.text:
Replace the LLM provider. Returns self.

from ragkit.models import OpenRouterProvider
agent.set_model(OpenRouterProvider(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"))

docs.title: .set_embedder(embedder) → Agent
docs.text:
Replace the embedding provider. Returns self.

from ragkit.embeddings import SentenceTransformerEmbedder
agent.set_embedder(SentenceTransformerEmbedder("paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2"))

docs.title: .set_store(store) → Agent
docs.text:
Replace the vector store. Returns self.

from ragkit.vectorstores import ChromaStore
agent.set_store(ChromaStore(collection_name="prod", persist_directory=".ragkit/chroma"))

docs.title: .install(plugin) → Agent
docs.text:
Register and activate a plugin.

from ragkit_observability import ObservabilityPlugin
agent.install(ObservabilityPlugin())

docs.title: .on(event) → decorator
docs.text:
Register a hook function for a lifecycle event.

@agent.on("before_generate")
def my_hook(prompt: str) -> None:
    ...

Available events: before_index, before_generate, after_generate, after_response.

docs.title: .index() → list[Document]
docs.text:
Load all sources, clean, chunk, embed, and store. Returns the list of indexed chunks.

chunks = agent.index()
print(f"Indexed {len(chunks)} chunks")

Warning

Calling index() without any .use() source returns an empty list and logs a warning.

docs.title: .sync() → list[Document]
docs.text:
Call source.sync() on all sources, then re-index. Returns chunks.

agent.sync()

docs.title: .ask(query) → str
docs.text:
Embed the query, retrieve context, build a prompt, and return the LLM's answer.

response = agent.ask("What is the refund policy?")

docs.title: .stream(query) → AsyncIterator[str]
docs.text:
Same as ask() but yields tokens as they stream from the LLM.

async for token in agent.stream("Explain the onboarding flow"):
    print(token, end="", flush=True)

docs.title: Document
docs.text:
The core data object passed through the pipeline.

from ragkit.core.base import Document

doc = Document(
    content="The text content.",
    metadata={"source": "file.md", "type": "markdown"},
    id=None,          # str | None — auto-generated during indexing
    embedding=None,   # list[float] | None — filled during indexing
)

docs.title: MarkdownSource(path)
docs.text: MarkdownSource(path: str | Path)

docs.title: TxtSource(path)
docs.text: TxtSource(path: str | Path)

docs.title: JsonSource(path, content_key="content")
docs.text: JsonSource(path: str | Path, content_key: str = "content")

docs.title: CsvSource(path, content_column=None)
docs.text: CsvSource(path: str | Path, content_column: str | None = None)

docs.title: RestSource(url, ...)
docs.text:
RestSource(
    url: str,
    content_key: str = "content",
    headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    timeout: int = 30,
)

Raises RestSourceError on network failure, HTTP error, or invalid JSON.

docs.title: DirectorySource(path, extensions=None)
docs.text:
DirectorySource(
    path: str | Path,
    extensions: set[str] | None = None,  # default: {".md", ".txt", ".json", ".csv"}
)

docs.title: SentenceTransformerEmbedder(model_name)
docs.text:
from ragkit.embeddings import SentenceTransformerEmbedder

embedder = SentenceTransformerEmbedder(
    model_name: str = "all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
)

Model is lazy-loaded on first call to .embed().

docs.title: ChromaStore(collection_name, persist_directory)
docs.text:
from ragkit.vectorstores import ChromaStore

store = ChromaStore(
    collection_name: str = "ragkit",
    persist_directory: str = ".ragkit/chroma",
)

docs.title: OpenRouterProvider(...)
docs.text:
from ragkit.models import OpenRouterProvider

provider = OpenRouterProvider(
    model: str = "openai/gpt-4o-mini",
    api_key: str | None = None,       # falls back to $OPENROUTER_API_KEY
    base_url: str | None = None,
    temperature: float = 0.7,
    max_tokens: int = 2048,
)

Raises OpenRouterError on API key issues, HTTP errors, or network failures.

docs.title: Base classes
docs.text: Implement these to create custom components:

docs.title: SourceAdapter
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.base import SourceAdapter, Document

class MySource(SourceAdapter):
    def load(self) -> list[Document]: ...
    def transform(self, docs: list[Document]) -> list[Document]: ...  # optional
    def sync(self) -> None: ...                                        # optional

docs.title: EmbeddingProvider
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.base import EmbeddingProvider

class MyEmbedder(EmbeddingProvider):
    def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: ...

docs.title: VectorStore
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.base import VectorStore, Document

class MyStore(VectorStore):
    def insert(self, docs: list[Document]) -> None: ...
    def search(self, query_vec: list[float], top_k: int = 5) -> list[Document]: ...
    def clear(self) -> None: ...  # optional

docs.title: ModelProvider
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.base import ModelProvider
from typing import AsyncIterator

class MyProvider(ModelProvider):
    def generate(self, prompt: str) -> str: ...
    async def stream(self, prompt: str) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...

docs.title: Plugin
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.base import Plugin

class MyPlugin(Plugin):
    def register(self, agent) -> None:
        agent.on("before_generate")(self.my_hook)

docs.title: ProjectConfig
docs.text:
from ragkit.core.config import ProjectConfig, ModelConfig, PipelineConfig, RetrievalConfig, CacheConfig

config = ProjectConfig(
    name="my-app",
    model=ModelConfig(provider="openrouter", model="openai/gpt-4o-mini"),
    pipeline=PipelineConfig(chunk_size=500, overlap=50),
    retrieval=RetrievalConfig(top_k=5, method="vector"),
    cache=CacheConfig(enabled=True, ttl=3600, directory=".ragkit"),
)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to RAGKit are documented here.

docs.title: [0.1.0] — 2026-06-27
docs.text: Initial public release.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
Core

  • Agent facade with fluent API: .use(), .configure(), .set_model(), .set_embedder(), .set_store(), .install(), .on()
  • index() — load, clean, chunk, embed, store
  • sync() — pull fresh data, then re-index
  • ask() — retrieve context and generate answer
  • stream() — async token streaming via AsyncIterator[str]

Sources

  • MarkdownSource — .md files and directories (recursive)
  • TxtSource — .txt files and directories
  • JsonSource — .json files, configurable content_key
  • CsvSource — .csv files, configurable content_column
  • RestSource — HTTP/JSON APIs with headers, params, timeout
  • DirectorySource — multi-format directory scanner

Pipeline

  • Cleaner — whitespace normalization
  • Chunker — boundary-aware splitting with configurable size and overlap

Embeddings

  • SentenceTransformerEmbedder — local model, lazy-loaded on first use
  • CachedEmbedder — disk-backed SHA-256 cache via diskcache

Vector stores

  • ChromaStore — persistent local Chroma collection, lazy client init

Models

  • OpenRouterProvider — OpenRouter API with sync generate and async streaming
  • Typed OpenRouterError for all HTTP and network failure modes

Cache

  • QueryCache — disk-backed LLM response cache with configurable TTL

Hooks

  • HookRegistry with @agent.on(event) decorator
  • Events: before_index, before_generate, after_generate, after_response

Plugin system

  • Plugin ABC
  • PluginRegistry with importlib.metadata entry_point discovery

Configuration

  • ProjectConfig (pydantic v2) with ModelConfig, PipelineConfig, RetrievalConfig, CacheConfig
  • pydantic-settings environment variable loading (.env + shell env)
  • rag init generates a rag.yaml starter config

CLI (via Typer)

  • rag init — scaffold a new project
  • rag index — index all sources
  • rag sync — sync sources and re-index
  • rag ask — one-shot query
  • rag dev — interactive REPL
  • rag serve — FastAPI HTTP server (/ask endpoint, requires ragkit[serve])
  • python -m ragkit entrypoint

Observability

  • structlog structured logging throughout the pipeline
  • Log events: index_called_without_sources, embedding hits/misses, query cache hits

CI

  • GitHub Actions: test matrix (Python 3.10/3.11/3.12), ruff lint, CLI smoke tests

Documentation

  • MkDocs Material site with dark mode, code copy, navigation tabs
  • Pages: Quickstart, Configuration, Sources, Pipeline, Models, CLI, Plugins, API Reference, Contributing

docs.title: [0.2.0] — planned
docs.text:
• Hybrid search (BM25 + vector)
  • Re-ranking (cross-encoder)
  • PineconeStore adapter
  • OpenAIEmbedder adapter
  • Web source (WebSource with HTML extraction)
  • Async index() for large corpora

docs.title: [0.3.0] — planned
docs.text:
• Multi-agent support (parallel indexing)
  • Evaluation harness (RAGAS integration)
  • REST API authentication
  • Streaming via SSE in rag serve

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text:
RAGKit ships with a full command-line interface. All commands read rag.yaml from the current directory by default.

rag --help
python -m ragkit --help   # equivalent

docs.title: rag init
docs.text:
Initialize a new RAGKit project in a directory.

rag init [DIRECTORY] [--name PROJECT_NAME]

Arguments:

Argument Default Description DIRECTORY . (current dir) Target directory --name my-rag-app Project name written to rag.yaml

Creates:

  • rag.yaml — project configuration
  • .env — environment variables file (if not already present)

Example:

mkdir my-app && rag init my-app --name my-app

docs.title: rag index
docs.text:
Load all sources and index them into the vector store.

rag index [--config PATH]
Option Default Description --config rag.yaml Path to config file

Run this after adding new documents or changing sources.

rag index
# Indexing sources...
# Indexed 142 chunks.

docs.title: rag sync
docs.text:
Sync all sources (calls source.sync()) then re-index.

rag sync [--config PATH]

Use sync when your sources can pull new data (e.g. a REST API with new records). For static files, sync and index are equivalent.

docs.title: rag ask
docs.text:
Ask a one-shot question and print the answer.

rag ask "QUERY" [--config PATH]

Example:

rag ask "What is the refund policy?"
# You can request a refund within 30 days of purchase...

docs.title: rag dev
docs.text:
Start an interactive REPL.

rag dev [--config PATH]

RAGKit dev mode. Type 'exit' to quit.

You: How does authentication work?
Agent: Authentication uses Bearer tokens...

You: What models are supported?
Agent: RAGKit supports any model available on OpenRouter...

You: exit

Type exit or quit to stop. Ctrl+C or EOF also exits cleanly.

docs.title: rag serve
docs.text:
Start an HTTP server with a /ask endpoint.

rag serve [--host HOST] [--port PORT] [--config PATH]
Option Default Description --host 127.0.0.1 Bind address --port 8000 Port --config rag.yaml Config file

Requires the serve extra:

pip install ragkit-sdk[serve]

Usage:

rag serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
# Starting RAGKit server on 0.0.0.0:8080

Query the API:

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/ask \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query": "How does authentication work?"}'

# {"response": "Authentication uses Bearer tokens..."}

docs.title: Global options
docs.text: Option Description --help Show help and exit --install-completion Install shell tab completion --show-completion Show completion script

docs.title: Using a custom config file
docs.text:
All commands accept --config:

rag index --config config/production.yaml
rag ask "hello" --config config/staging.yaml

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: RAGKit is configured via two files: rag.yaml for project settings and .env for secrets.

docs.title: rag.yaml
docs.text:
Full reference with all available options:

project:
  name: my-app              # Project name (used in logs)

model:
  provider: openrouter      # Only supported provider (required)
  model: openai/gpt-4o-mini # Model slug from OpenRouter catalog

pipeline:
  chunk_size: 500           # Max characters per chunk
  overlap: 50               # Overlap between consecutive chunks

retrieval:
  top_k: 5                  # Number of chunks retrieved per query
  method: vector            # "vector" (default) | "hybrid" (coming V0.2)

cache:
  enabled: true             # Enable embedding + query cache
  ttl: 3600                 # Cache TTL in seconds (1 hour default)
  directory: .ragkit        # Root directory for all cache data

docs.title: Defaults
docs.text:
If rag.yaml is absent or a key is omitted, RAGKit uses these defaults:

Key Default model.model openai/gpt-4o-mini pipeline.chunk_size 500 pipeline.overlap 50 retrieval.top_k 5 cache.enabled true cache.ttl 3600 cache.directory .ragkit

docs.title: Environment variables (.env)
docs.text:
Variable Required Description OPENROUTER_API_KEY Yes Your OpenRouter API key OPENROUTER_BASE_URL No Override the API base URL (default: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1)

RAGKit automatically loads .env from the current directory via pydantic-settings. You can also export variables directly in your shell.

export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-...

docs.title: Programmatic configuration
docs.text:
You can bypass rag.yaml entirely and configure the agent in Python:

from ragkit import Agent
from ragkit.core.config import ProjectConfig

config = ProjectConfig()
config.model.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"
config.pipeline.chunk_size = 300
config.retrieval.top_k = 8
config.cache.enabled = False

agent = Agent(config=config)

Or load a custom config file:

agent = Agent(config="path/to/my-config.yaml")

docs.title: Cache directory layout
docs.text:
All cache and index data is stored under cache.directory (default .ragkit):

.ragkit/
├── chroma/         ← Chroma vector store (persisted)
├── embeddings/     ← Embedding cache (diskcache SQLite)
└── queries/        ← Query response cache (diskcache SQLite)

To reset completely:

rm -rf .ragkit/
rag index

docs.title: Multiple environments
docs.text:
Use different config files per environment:

rag index --config rag.prod.yaml
rag ask "..." --config rag.staging.yaml

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: RAGKit is open source under the MIT License. Contributions of all kinds are welcome.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/your-org/ragkit.git
cd ragkit
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
ragkit/
├── agent.py              # Agent facade (main entry point)
├── cli/
│   └── main.py           # Typer CLI commands
├── core/
│   ├── base.py           # ABCs: SourceAdapter, EmbeddingProvider, VectorStore, ModelProvider, Plugin
│   ├── config.py         # ProjectConfig (pydantic), Settings (pydantic-settings)
│   ├── hooks.py          # HookRegistry
│   └── registry.py       # PluginRegistry (entry_points discovery)
├── sources/              # Built-in source adapters
├── embeddings/           # Embedding providers
├── vectorstores/         # Vector store implementations
├── models/               # LLM providers
├── pipeline/             # Chunker, Cleaner
└── cache/                # CachedEmbedder, QueryCache
tests/
├── unit/                 # Unit tests (all dependencies mocked)
└── scripts/              # Bash smoke tests
docs/                     # MkDocs documentation source

docs.title: Running the tests
docs.text:
# All unit tests
pytest tests/unit/ -v

# With coverage
pytest tests/unit/ --cov=ragkit --cov-report=term-missing

# Bash smoke tests (CLI must be installed)
bash tests/scripts/test_cli_smoke.sh

# Embeddings smoke test (requires sentence-transformers)
bash tests/scripts/test_embeddings_smoke.sh

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
We use ruff for linting and formatting:

# Check
ruff check .

# Auto-fix
ruff check . --fix

# Format
ruff format .

docs.title: Adding a source
docs.text:
1. Create ragkit/sources/my_source.py
  2. Implement SourceAdapter from ragkit.core.base
  3. Export from ragkit/sources/__init__.py
  4. Export from ragkit/__init__.py
  5. Add unit tests in tests/unit/test_sources.py
  6. Document in docs/sources.md

Minimal example:

from pathlib import Path
from ragkit.core.base import SourceAdapter, Document

class RstSource(SourceAdapter):
    def __init__(self, path: str | Path) -> None:
        self._path = Path(path)

    def load(self) -> list[Document]:
        files = [self._path] if self._path.is_file() else list(self._path.rglob("*.rst"))
        docs = []
        for f in files:
            docs.append(Document(
                content=f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
                metadata={"source": str(f.resolve()), "type": "rst"},
            ))
        return docs

docs.title: Adding a model provider
docs.text:
1. Create ragkit/models/my_provider.py
  2. Implement ModelProvider from ragkit.core.base
  3. Export from ragkit/models/__init__.py
  4. Add unit tests
  5. Document in docs/models.md

docs.title: Pull request checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Tests pass: pytest tests/unit/ -v
  • [ ] Linter clean: ruff check .
  • [ ] New code is covered by tests
  • [ ] Public API documented in the relevant docs/*.md page
  • [ ] Commit message is descriptive

docs.title: Reporting bugs
docs.text:
Open an issue on GitHub with:

  • RAGKit version (pip show ragkit)
  • Python version (python --version)
  • Steps to reproduce
  • Expected vs actual behavior

docs.title: License
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the MIT License.

docs.title: Models
docs.text: RAGKit generates answers via OpenRouter — a unified API that gives access to 100+ LLMs from a single endpoint.

docs.title: Why OpenRouter?
docs.text:
• One API key for GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, and more
  • Competitive pricing — pay per token, no subscriptions
  • Fallback routing — automatic failover between providers
  • Free tier — several models available at no cost

docs.title: Default model
docs.text:
RAGKit defaults to openai/gpt-4o-mini — a fast and affordable model suitable for most RAG use cases.

Change it in rag.yaml:

model:
  provider: openrouter
  model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5

Or in Python:

from ragkit.models import OpenRouterProvider

agent.set_model(OpenRouterProvider(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"))

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
from ragkit.models import OpenRouterProvider

provider = OpenRouterProvider(
    model="openai/gpt-4o",
    api_key="sk-or-v1-...",    # or set OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env
    temperature=0.3,            # 0.0 = deterministic, 1.0 = creative
    max_tokens=2048,            # max response length
)

agent.set_model(provider)
Parameter Default Description model openai/gpt-4o-mini Model slug api_key $OPENROUTER_API_KEY API key temperature 0.7 Creativity (0–1) max_tokens 2048 Max response tokens

docs.title: Recommended models
docs.text:
Use case Model Notes Default / budget openai/gpt-4o-mini Fast, cheap, reliable Best quality anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 Best for long context Open source meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct Free on many providers Coding openai/gpt-4o Strong reasoning Multilingual google/gemini-2.0-flash-exp 100+ languages Free mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct:free No cost, limited rate

Browse the full catalog at openrouter.ai/models.

docs.title: Streaming
docs.text:
import asyncio

async def main():
    agent = Agent()
    agent.use(MarkdownSource("./docs"))
    agent.index()

    print("Answer: ", end="", flush=True)
    async for token in agent.stream("Explain the architecture"):
        print(token, end="", flush=True)
    print()

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
OpenRouterProvider raises OpenRouterError for actionable failures:

from ragkit.models.openrouter import OpenRouterError

try:
    response = agent.ask("hello")
except OpenRouterError as e:
    print(f"LLM error: {e}")
Situation Error message Empty API key OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not set. Invalid key (401) Invalid API key (401). Rate limit (429) Rate limit exceeded (429). Retry later. Timeout Request timed out after 60s. Network down Network error: ...

docs.title: Custom model provider
docs.text:
Implement ModelProvider to use any LLM:

from ragkit.core.base import ModelProvider
from typing import AsyncIterator

class MyProvider(ModelProvider):
    def generate(self, prompt: str) -> str:
        # call your LLM
        return my_llm.complete(prompt)

    async def stream(self, prompt: str) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        async for token in my_llm.stream(prompt):
            yield token

agent.set_model(MyProvider())

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text: The RAGKit pipeline transforms raw documents into searchable vector chunks, then retrieves and generates answers at query time.

docs.title: 1. Load
docs.text: Each source's load() method returns a list of Document objects with raw text and metadata.

docs.title: 2. Clean
docs.text:
The Cleaner normalizes text before chunking:

  • Collapses 3+ consecutive newlines into 2
  • Collapses multiple spaces into one
  • Strips leading/trailing whitespace

docs.title: 3. Chunk
docs.text:
The Chunker splits long documents into overlapping segments.

agent.configure(
    chunk_size=500,   # max characters per chunk
    overlap=50,       # overlap between consecutive chunks
)

Chunking strategy: RAGKit tries to break at sentence (.) or paragraph (\n) boundaries. If no natural boundary is found, it breaks at the last space.

Example: A 1200-character document with chunk_size=500, overlap=50 produces:

Chunk 1: chars 0–500
Chunk 2: chars 450–950    (50 char overlap)
Chunk 3: chars 900–1200

Each chunk carries the source document's metadata plus a chunk_index.

docs.title: 4. Embed
docs.text:
The Embedder converts each chunk's text into a dense vector.

Default: SentenceTransformerEmbedder("all-MiniLM-L6-v2") — a lightweight local model (~90MB, 384-dimensional embeddings).

from ragkit.embeddings import SentenceTransformerEmbedder

agent.set_embedder(SentenceTransformerEmbedder("paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2"))

The model is lazy-loaded on first use and cached in memory for subsequent calls.

docs.title: 5. Store
docs.text:
Embedded chunks are upserted into the vector store with their text and metadata.

from ragkit.vectorstores import ChromaStore

agent.set_store(ChromaStore(
    collection_name="my-project",
    persist_directory=".ragkit/chroma",
))

docs.title: 1. Embed the query
docs.text: The user's question is embedded with the same model used at index time.

docs.title: 2. Retrieve
docs.text:
Top-k most similar chunks are retrieved by cosine similarity.

agent.configure(top_k=5)   # retrieve 5 chunks

docs.title: 3. Build the prompt
docs.text:
RAGKit constructs a prompt with the retrieved context:

Use the following context to answer the question.

Context:
[chunk 1 content]

---

[chunk 2 content]

---

[chunk 3 content]

Question: <your question>

Answer:

docs.title: 4. Generate
docs.text: The prompt is sent to OpenRouter. The response is returned as a string or streamed token by token.

docs.title: Cache
docs.text:
Two caching layers reduce latency and cost:

Cache What it stores Key Embedding cache Vector for a given text SHA-256 of text Query cache LLM response for a query + context SHA-256 of query + context

Both caches are disk-based (diskcache SQLite), persistent across runs, and stored under cache.directory.

# Disable cache
from ragkit.core.config import ProjectConfig
config = ProjectConfig()
config.cache.enabled = False
agent = Agent(config=config)

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Intercept any stage of the pipeline:

@agent.on("before_index")
def on_before_index(docs):
    print(f"About to index {len(docs)} documents")

@agent.on("before_generate")
def on_before_generate(prompt):
    print(f"Prompt length: {len(prompt)} chars")

@agent.on("after_generate")
def on_after_generate(response):
    print(f"Response: {response[:80]}...")

@agent.on("after_response")
def on_after_response(response):
    # Final hook — after cache write
    save_to_db(response)

Available hooks:

Hook When Arguments before_index Before indexing starts docs: list[Document] before_generate Before LLM call prompt: str after_generate After LLM returns response: str after_response After cache write response: str

docs.title: Plugin Development
docs.text: RAGKit's plugin system lets you extend the framework and share reusable integrations with the community.

docs.title: What plugins can do
docs.text:
A plugin receives the Agent instance at registration time and can:

  • Register hooks (before_index, before_generate, after_generate, after_response)
  • Swap components (set_model, set_embedder, set_store)
  • Add custom sources
  • Wrap existing methods

docs.title: Writing a plugin
docs.text:
Implement the Plugin ABC from ragkit.core.base:

from ragkit.core.base import Plugin

class LoggingPlugin(Plugin):
    def register(self, agent) -> None:
        agent.on("before_generate")(self._log_prompt)
        agent.on("after_response")(self._log_response)

    def _log_prompt(self, prompt: str) -> None:
        print(f"[LoggingPlugin] Prompt ({len(prompt)} chars)")

    def _log_response(self, response: str) -> None:
        print(f"[LoggingPlugin] Response ({len(response)} chars)")

Install it:

from ragkit import Agent
from my_package import LoggingPlugin

agent = Agent()
agent.install(LoggingPlugin())

docs.title: Available hooks
docs.text: Event When Argument before_index Before indexing documents docs: list[Document] before_generate Before calling the LLM prompt: str after_generate After LLM returns a response response: str after_response After cache write, final step response: str

docs.title: Distributing as a PyPI package
docs.text:
Structure your plugin as a standard Python package:

ragkit-logging/
├── pyproject.toml
└── ragkit_logging/
    └── __init__.py

pyproject.toml:

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "ragkit-logging"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Structured logging plugin for RAGKit"
dependencies = ["ragkit>=0.1.0"]

[project.entry-points."ragkit.plugins"]
logging = "ragkit_logging:LoggingPlugin"

The [project.entry-points."ragkit.plugins"] section lets RAGKit auto-discover your plugin.

docs.title: Auto-discovery
docs.text:
RAGKit scans the ragkit.plugins entry point group on startup. Plugins registered there are available via the PluginRegistry:

from ragkit.core.registry import PluginRegistry

registry = PluginRegistry()
registry.discover()         # scans installed packages

for name, plugin_class in registry.plugins.items():
    print(f"Found plugin: {name}")
    agent.install(plugin_class())

Manual install (without auto-discovery):

from ragkit_logging import LoggingPlugin
agent.install(LoggingPlugin())

docs.title: Example: custom source plugin
docs.text:
A plugin can register a source adapter from a third-party library:

from ragkit.core.base import Plugin, SourceAdapter, Document

class NotionPlugin(Plugin):
    def __init__(self, database_id: str, token: str) -> None:
        self.database_id = database_id
        self.token = token

    def register(self, agent) -> None:
        agent.use(NotionSource(self.database_id, self.token))


class NotionSource(SourceAdapter):
    def __init__(self, database_id: str, token: str) -> None:
        self.database_id = database_id
        self.token = token

    def load(self) -> list[Document]:
        import httpx
        resp = httpx.get(
            f"https://api.notion.com/v1/databases/{self.database_id}/query",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}", "Notion-Version": "2022-06-28"},
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        pages = resp.json().get("results", [])
        return [
            Document(
                content=page["properties"]["content"]["rich_text"][0]["plain_text"],
                metadata={"id": page["id"], "source": "notion"},
            )
            for page in pages
            if page["properties"].get("content")
        ]

Usage:

agent.install(NotionPlugin(database_id="abc123", token="secret-token"))
agent.index()

docs.title: Community plugins
docs.text:
Package Description ragkit-pdf PDF file source (requires pypdf) ragkit-docx Word document source (requires python-docx) ragkit-notion Notion database source ragkit-github GitHub repository loader ragkit-slack Slack channel history

Install any plugin via pip and it's ready to use — no configuration required.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get your first RAGKit agent running in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • An OpenRouter API key (free tier available)

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install ragkit-sdk

docs.title: 2. Initialize a project
docs.text:
mkdir my-rag-app
cd my-rag-app
rag init

This creates two files:

my-rag-app/
├── rag.yaml    ← project configuration
└── .env        ← your API keys (never commit this)

docs.title: 3. Add your API key
docs.text:
Open .env and set your OpenRouter key:

OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-...

Get a free key at openrouter.ai/keys.

docs.title: 4. Add your data
docs.text:
Create a docs/ folder and drop in some Markdown files:

mkdir docs
echo "# Authentication\nUse Bearer tokens in the Authorization header." > docs/auth.md
echo "# Pricing\nFree tier: 1000 requests/month. Pro: unlimited." > docs/pricing.md

docs.title: 5. Index your data
docs.text:
rag index

RAGKit will:

  1. Load all Markdown files from docs/
  2. Clean and chunk the text
  3. Generate embeddings (downloads a small model on first run, ~100MB)
  4. Store vectors in .ragkit/chroma/

docs.title: 6. Ask a question
docs.text:
rag ask "How do I authenticate?"

Or start an interactive session:

rag dev

RAGKit dev mode. Type 'exit' to quit.

You: How do I authenticate?
Agent: Use Bearer tokens in the Authorization header.

You: What's the pricing?
Agent: The free tier includes 1000 requests per month. The Pro plan offers unlimited requests.

You: exit

docs.title: 7. Use the Python API
docs.text:
from ragkit import Agent, MarkdownSource

agent = Agent()
agent.use(MarkdownSource("./docs"))
agent.index()

response = agent.ask("How do I authenticate?")
print(response)

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Configuration — customize rag.yaml
  • Sources — add CSV, REST APIs, JSON data
  • Models — switch to GPT-4o, Claude, or Llama
  • CLI — full CLI reference

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
The model download takes too long

On the first run, RAGKit downloads the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding model (~90MB). This only happens once. Subsequent runs use the cached version.

I get a 401 error

Your OPENROUTER_API_KEY is missing or invalid. Check that your .env file contains the key and that it is correct.

rag: command not found

Make sure you installed RAGKit in an active virtual environment: 

python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install ragkit-sdk
rag --help
Or run via Python: python -m ragkit --help

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
Sources are adapters that load data into RAGKit. All sources implement the same interface and can be combined freely.

agent.use(source_a)
agent.use(source_b)
agent.index()   # indexes all sources

docs.title: MarkdownSource
docs.text:
Loads .md files from a file or directory (recursive).

from ragkit import MarkdownSource

# Single file
agent.use(MarkdownSource("./README.md"))

# All .md files in a folder (recursive)
agent.use(MarkdownSource("./docs"))

Metadata added to each document:

Key Value source Absolute file path type "markdown"

docs.title: TxtSource
docs.text:
Loads .txt files.

from ragkit import TxtSource

agent.use(TxtSource("./transcripts"))

docs.title: JsonSource
docs.text:
Loads .json files. Supports arrays and nested objects.

from ragkit import JsonSource

# Use a specific key as the document content
agent.use(JsonSource("./articles.json", content_key="body"))

# Without content_key: entire object serialized as content
agent.use(JsonSource("./data.json"))

Example JSON: 

[
  { "body": "Article content here", "title": "My Article", "date": "2026-01-01" },
  { "body": "Another article", "title": "Second Article", "date": "2026-01-02" }
]

The body field becomes the document content; title and date go into metadata.

docs.title: CsvSource
docs.text:
Loads .csv files row by row.

from ragkit import CsvSource

# Use a specific column as content; other columns go to metadata
agent.use(CsvSource("./faq.csv", content_column="answer"))

# Without content_column: all columns joined as "key: value | key: value"
agent.use(CsvSource("./products.csv"))

Example CSV: 

question,answer,category
"How do I reset my password?","Go to Settings > Security.","account"

docs.title: RestSource
docs.text:
Fetches data from an HTTP API.

from ragkit import RestSource

agent.use(RestSource(
    url="https://api.example.com/articles",
    content_key="content",          # JSON key to use as document content
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer token"},
    params={"limit": "100"},
    timeout=30,                     # seconds
))

The endpoint must return either a JSON object or an array of objects.

Error handling: RestSource raises RestSourceError with a clear message on:

  • Network failure
  • HTTP 4xx/5xx response
  • Non-JSON response body
  • Timeout

docs.title: DirectorySource
docs.text:
Scans a directory and loads all supported file types.

from ragkit import DirectorySource

# Default: loads .md, .txt, .json, .csv
agent.use(DirectorySource("./data"))

# Custom extensions
agent.use(DirectorySource("./data", extensions={".md", ".rst"}))

Silently skips files that cannot be decoded as UTF-8.

docs.title: Custom sources
docs.text:
Implement SourceAdapter to add any data source:

from ragkit.core.base import SourceAdapter, Document

class NotionSource(SourceAdapter):
    def __init__(self, database_id: str, token: str) -> None:
        self.database_id = database_id
        self.token = token

    def load(self) -> list[Document]:
        # Call Notion API, return list of Document
        ...
        return [
            Document(content=page["content"], metadata={"id": page["id"]})
            for page in pages
        ]

    def sync(self) -> None:
        # Optional: pull latest changes before indexing
        pass

agent.use(NotionSource(database_id="...", token="..."))

docs.title: Plugin sources
docs.text:
Community-maintained sources are distributed as pip packages:

pip install ragkit-pdf      # PDF files
pip install ragkit-docx     # Word documents
pip install ragkit-notion   # Notion databases
pip install ragkit-github   # GitHub repos

After installation, plugins are automatically discovered:

from ragkit_pdf import PDFSource
agent.use(PDFSource("./documents"))

docs.title: The Document object
docs.text:
All sources produce Document instances:

from ragkit.core.base import Document

doc = Document(
    content="The main text content.",
    metadata={"source": "file.md", "type": "markdown"},
    id=None,          # auto-generated by the pipeline
    embedding=None,   # filled during indexing
)
